title: Header Fields, Representations, Negotiation, Validators, and Caching · Go Semantics Case: Content Type Accept Negotiation
summary: Clients send repeated and malformed media fields, the server sniffs content, omits Vary, caches personalized responses, and computes validators from unstable JSON ordering. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo content type accept negotiation increment in representation selection and cache contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate request Content-Type and parameters, select an acceptable response representation, and return 415 or 406 with a documented default.
Handle case-insensitive field names, repeated values, list syntax, hop-by-hop fields, size limits, and canonical map behavior without naïve splitting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Content Type Accept Negotiation · Theory Content Type Accept Negotiation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9471 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to negotiate a representation and keep validator and privacy decisions independent; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Content Type Accept Negotiation contract for the supplied data.
